description: Apollo 10 Flight Journal - Day 5, part 22: Snoopy and Charlie Brown are hugging each other

tokens: The LM DSEA was not recording whilst the LM was behind the Moon and out of MSFN contact, so no onboard voice recording exists during the final minutes of the rendezvous or the docking.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Flight Plan page 3-63.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: CSM solo operations detailed Flight Plan
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The second midcourse correction was carried out at 105:52:56 (RCS) X, -0.8; Y, +1.5; Z, +1.7; total, +1.84 (all fps: X, -0.24; Y, +0.46; Z, +0.52; total, +0.56 m/s)
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The second midcourse correction fine-tuned the final approach between the LM Snoopy and the CSM Charlie Brown.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The crew commented on the second midcourse correction during the post mission debrief.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"For the second (midcourse correction) one, we got plus 0.8, out-of-plane was plus 1.4, and minus 0.8 (all fps) (X, +0.24; Y, +0.43; Z, -0.24 m/s).
The first midcourse gave us minus 0.4 (fps, 0.12 m/s).
The second midcourse gave us plus 1.4 (fps, 0.43 m/s), and then, when we were coming into the final braking, we saw some resultant out-of-plane coming into the final braking.
But I don't think there's anything else to say about the midcourse, except that the backup solutions were made.
They agreed fairly closely with the PGNS.
The CSM agreed, and we burned them.
The CSM agreed almost number for number in most cases with the LGC.
We plotted right up the pike; we were plotting the proper ranges and the proper range rates, and the elevation angle was within a pencil width right up the nominal track up to braking."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The LM then entered the Terminal Phase Final phase of the rendezvous, which consists of a series of 4 small braking gate RCS burns.

They nominally occur at:6,000 feet (1,829 m) reduction in range rate 30 fps (9.1 m/s)3,000 feet (914 m) reduction in range rate 20 fps (6.1 m/s)1,500 feet (457 m) reduction in range rate 10 fps (3.0 m/s)500 feet (152 m) reduction in range rate 5 fps (1.5 m/s)The braking was completed at 106:05:49 with a combined Delta-V of X, +18.5; Y, -2.6; Z, +25.5; total, +36.6 (all fps: X, +5.6; Y, -0.8; Z, +7.8; total, +11.2 m/s)
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The rendezvous radar is used to monitor the LM closing rates, which are then adjusted at the braking gates, to eventually match exactly the CSM orbit.
The crew use P47 to monitor the changes in velocity at the braking gates and also display the range and range rate to the CSM.
At the conclusion of these braking manoeuvres, the LM was station keeping with the CSM, the LM leading the CSM in its orbit.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: During the braking manoeuvres, the CMP uses P79 to make the Automaneuver to alignment between the two spacecraft in preparation for docking.
Once the spacecraft are aligned, P79 displays the range, range rate and angular difference between the CSM's X-axis and the LM.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The handling characteristics of the lightweight LM during the braking were slightly more sensitive than those experienced in the simulator.
Oscillations were evident, and thruster firings were noticeably more frequent than during the simulations.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Following a few minutes of station keeping, for photography of each spacecraft, the LM pitched down to present the docking drogue and target to the CSM.
The LM was now placed in AGS Att Hold and Min Deadband.
The CSM now became the active vehicle for the final docking.
The CMP thrusted forward at approximately 1.0 fps (0.3 m/s) initially to close in on the LM.
He slowed the closing rate so that it eventually was only a small fraction of a foot per second.
Using the COAS he aligned the CSM with the docking target on the LM.
This also aligned the docking probe with the docking drogue on the LM.

The extended docking probe finally made its initial soft contact between the two spacecraft and the three capture latches in the tip of the probe engaged in the hole at the bottom of the conical drogue.
Pneumatic shock absorbers in the probe dampened the motion between the two spacecraft.
The CMP observed a talkback indicator in the CSM signalling capture of the LM.

Once any attitude excursions between the two spacecraft had been damped out by the CMP, he used the probe retract switch on panel 2 to electronically retract the probe and bring the two spacecraft together so that the twelve docking latches could engage and make a hard dock.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The crew commented on the terminal braking during the post mission debrief.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"As we approached 1 nautical mile (1.85 km), we had approximately 32 fps (range rate, 10.7 m/s).
Our braking gate calls for 30 fps (9.1 m/s) at 1 nautical mile (1.85 km).
I could not see braking just for 2 fps (0.61 m/s) at that time, particular since our angular rates were zero.
We continued on in.
The range rate was reduced according to our braking gates at 3,000 feet (900 m).
The minor out-of-plane was corrected at approximately 8,000 feet (2,400 m)."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"The Command Module was maneuvered to X-axis track, and it arrived there with the Lunar Module at about 3.5 miles (6.5 km) in the center of the COAS, between the width of the lines.
It was absolutely incredible.
It was about 2.5 degrees high, and it continued to drift up a little, just like it's supposed to.
It was beautiful all the way."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"The total radar performance was fantastic.

The calibration curves we obtained from the contractor for the AGC strength versus range match exactly, like the small card I had in front of us, which gave us a total correlation of the regime we were operating in and the indication that it would not be locked on a side lobe.
The inertial angle needles followed prescribed nominal approach.
At about 27 minutes, Alpha-dot went to zero.
We did have one small out-of-plane when thrusting was made, and the inertial and the vertical needle went to zero and from there we continued in.
At ½ mile (0.9 km), the range rate was reduced to 19 fps (5.8 m/s).
At ¼ mile, we reduced further on down, and finally we arrived at 500 feet (152 m) with 4 to 5 fps (1.2 to 1.5 m/s).
During this time, we continually made comparisons between the VHF ranging and the radar ranging on the Lunar Module.
It was apparent that they were in close agreement in every case.
In the final part, we were able to call ranges in the Command Module down to 300 feet (91 m), 180 feet (55 m), and get precise comparisons with the Lunar Module.
It appeared that the equipment was working most satisfactorily."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Flight Plan page 3-64.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: 16-mm sequence camera view of the CSM from the LM during the braking phase.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: H.264 MOV video file.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: 16-mm sequence camera view of the LM from the CSM during the final station keeping and positioning for docking.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: H.264 MOV video file.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 106 hours, 18 minutes into the mission.
We're about a minute away from acquisition ofCharlie Brown.Snoopyacquisition to come 15, 16 seconds after that ofCharlie Brown.
The two spacecraft should be station keeping as we acquire them.
On the sixteenth revolution here, we have the capability on both this revolution number 16 and on revolution number 17 to receive television.
Television is scheduled for the 17 revolution during the Ascent Propulsion System burn depletion.
However, the capability does exist to receive it, and it's possible we might have an unscheduled transmission.
We're not sure.
We should have AOS now onCharlie Brownany second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Okay.
Do you want it to come back to you?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
How do you read me?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: So far it looks good.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Hey, Joe.
We're about ready to dock.
Stand by.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Very good.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Don't call us.
We'll call you.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Roger that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, John.
You're in to about 5 feet, babe.
Looking beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: How far?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: [Garble] feet.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Got a capture?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yes.
Thrusters are Off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got a capture, John.
Fire when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Everything looks good in here Tom.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, babe?
Ullage looks good.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Solid as a rock in the cabin, babe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Following capture there was no significant attitude changes, the two spacecraft stayed very stable in relation to each other.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: All right, babe.
I can see you moving over.
Hit it.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Stafford is encouraging Young to move the Docking Probe Extd/Rel switch on panel 2 to the Retract position, to command the docking probe to electronically retract to bring the two spacecraft solidly together, in a hard dock.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Oh, we got them.
Right on the ball.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: [Garble] We got them, John.
We heard them in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The ripple fire of the twelve docking latches around the circumference of the docking ring, can be heard very loudly in both spacecraft as hard dock is achieved.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Hello, Houston.
Snoopy and Charlie Brown are hugging each other.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Roger that.
We heard them down here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: The crew commented on the station keeping and docking during the post mission debrief.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"We reached a relative position of zero R-dot at approximately 25 feet (7.6 m).
At that time, we decided since we were out in daylight to go right ahead to the docking phase and not waste any time.

I used Pulse, pitched over 90 degrees, had the Command and Service Module in the overhead window, then rolled to an attitude that I approximated from there on, the Command Module directed the Lunar Module exactly what attitudes to go to.
Then I went to AGS Attitude Hold with a tight deadband, and noted the rate error needles were off of zero deflection.
We were closing within 10 feet (3 m) when we had acquisition and MSFN.
I directed MSFN to stand by, that we were 10 feet (3 m) closing."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"I was trying to save a little gas, so I was trying to maneuver in pitch, roll, and yaw to line up, just holding station out there, and trying to direct Tom to the line-up on the crossbar.
It worked pretty good.
I got in about 2 or 3 feet (0.6 to 0.9 m) of having the crossbar all lined up.
Unfortunately, every maneuver I'd make after that just seemed to make things worse.
So, I finally gave up and went on in to dock."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Docking was the same as before.
It appeared to be no problem, although it was evident that the Lunar Module was a little more sporty as far as holding attitude.
But, it seemed to hold attitude very well, and it was no problem to line the crossbar and go on in and dock.
I used AGS Attitude Hold, Minimum Deadband."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Right at contact, I gave the word and Tom thrusted forward (plus-X upward); we got a capture."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"John hit the drogue right in the dead center.
There was never a mark on that whole drogue."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Capture from the LM was very evident.
Retraction was not, but when those latches went, it was extremely evident."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"It really was loud.
It was a lot louder than the one that we did from the Command Module."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Maybe it's because we had a pressurized LM this time."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"It was a lot louder the second time."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Of course, we had our helmets off."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"No, I had my helmet on."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"We had them on, babe.
You had a big hatch there, but you were punching holes in our drogue and we were not about to leave our helmets off."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Our procedure - as soon as capture occurred there were a couple of minor thrustings until we killed both attitude control systems."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"We were just partying."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Stafford, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Then John damped the maneuver.
A loud ripple bang was heard as the latches latched."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"A sigh of relief was given by all."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: From theApollo 10 Mission Report:"Docking was performed with the Command Module in autopilot control, and minimal thruster firing was required.
The alignment sight reticle washed out because of reflected sunlight from the Lunar Module at distances between 25 and 10 feet.
Docking could be done using only the plus-X thrusters of the Lunar Module to ensure capture.
There were no significant post-contact dynamics and no apparent interface attitude changes.
Completion of the retraction sequence was characterized by the reassuring sound of the automatic latches retracting."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Let's stay - let's stay in our helmets, babe, until we get this thing squared away.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, John.
That was beautiful.
Just beautiful, babe.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Okay.
Now, John, let me ask you one thing.
Do you want me to pressurize that LM tunnel through our hatch to save you from blowing that Mylar out again?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Again, the tunnel could not be vented; therefore, the Command Module was pressurized with the repressurization tank to approximately 5.4 psia to ensure tunnel-hatch pressure integrity.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Do you want us to pressurize the tunnel?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Man, we is back home.
Almost.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Okay, I'll get started, and you can start helping him go through the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, were you calling?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Negative, Gene.
We're standing by until you got some time.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Joe.
It's nominal that the rendezvous was the best one we've ever had.
Right up the pike all the way.
We'll talk about it later.
I'm going start going through the tail end of the activation checklist for the APS burn and depletion, and Tom and John will start on the tunnel.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
One thing we would like for you to do is go to secondary on the CO2canister.
We'd like to monitor that one while you're getting cleaned up there.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: You're right, Jose.
It's been a long day.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Say again, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger, Gene-o.
We'd like for you to go to secondary on CO2canister.
We want to monitor that canister while you're getting cleaned up and back in the Command Module.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Yes.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Stand by, Joe.
I can't - Wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Cernan is struggling to hear MCC-H due to the activities Stafford and Young are carrying out, pressurizing the docking tunnel, testing the pressure integrity of the seal between the two spacecraft, opening the LM overhead and CSM forward hatches.
The onboard communication between these crew members (not recorded) is disturbing Cernan.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Now go ahead, Houston.
Say again your last.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger.
Go secondary on CO2canister.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
We're secondary on CO2canister now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The LM Lithium Hydroxide (LiOH) canister was used to scrub carbon dioxide from the cabin atmosphere and suit loop.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Two aspects of the indicated carbon dioxide level measured were considered anomalous.
First, the rate of carbon dioxide increase from 97 to 101 hours GET exceeded the predicted levels by a factor of approximately 8.
Secondly, the level remained constant for the next 5 hours.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Post flight tests of the flight cartridge showed some indications exist that the flight cartridge was not reacting chemically as uniformly as a sample test cartridge.
This was probably because of variations in moisture content.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Roger.
Thank you.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, John.
How do you want to work the tunnel?
Do you want to pressurize it or do you want me to?
Okay.
We got plenty of pressure.
Okay.
That's better.
Okay.
Go ahead.
You pressurize it.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Hello, Houston, this is Snoopy.

One thing, Charlie Brown is getting ready to pressurize the tunnel, and we want to make sure we're in the right attitude and everything for the next maneuver as far as the next thing that he needs to know are the angles.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Okay.
We'll get them for you, Snoopy.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
Charlie Brown, Snoopy, this is Houston.
Your CSM gimbal angles are: roll, 300; pitch, 021; and yaw, 000.
We're standing by for your readback.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Roger that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Do you have an update on the LM weight?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Charlie Brown, this is Houston - No.
Snoopy, this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Go ahead.
Do you have an update on the LM weight?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've got that through, Gene-o, but we want you to load in your DAP 10010 for system A.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: You want 10 - Say again what you want.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
I want 10011.
That'll be system A.
That's in for your DAP; and, also, your LM weight for burn is 7 - 07544.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Roger.
LM weight is 7544.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what I thought, I didn't enter that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Joe, give me that once more and I'll get it straight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gene-o.
Your LM weight is 7544 and in your DAP we want 10011.
This is instead of 11011.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: The LM DAP code is entered in to the PGNS via routine 03.
The code is only entered in register 1 as values corresponding to the ABCDE values in the matrix below.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: MCC-H need to change the pre-planned DAP for the APS burn to depletion manoeuvre.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Now require a DAP set to 10011, which translates as, 1 = LM Ascent stage only, 0 = 2-jet translation RCS system A, 0 = Fine scaling ACA (4°/sec), 1 = Attitude deadband 1.0°, 1 = KALCMANU rate 0.5°/sec.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The only change is the RCS translation is now using 2 jets on system A rather than system B.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Very good.
We copy, Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Tom, is your stop button reset Auto for this?
I am going to wait on a couple of these things while I verify that that tunnel is clear.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Charlie Brown, Snoop.
Let me know [garble] PAD.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Roger, Snoopy.
Whenever you are ready, we can uplink your LM state vector to you.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
We're reader right now.
As soon as I get into P00 again.
Got everything.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
Thank you.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hello, Charlie Brown, this is Snoopy.
Let me know how the tunnel is coming.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Yes.
We're holding it.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Let me get my gloves off, babe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Good show, Tunnel all Tunnel all pressurized and everything looks good.
Okay.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Okay.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: [Garble] put our bags?
Do you remember?
Bags asked for these?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger, John.
We haven't moved at all.
Okay.
And Gene got the angles for that Auto maneuver.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: The Automaneuver angles Stafford is referring to are for the CSM to go to the APS burn to depletion attitude, in which the two spacecraft will separate.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Yes.
Most all of it is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Young is busy now removing the docking probe once the docking tunnel had been pressurized and the pressure integrity verified.
The docking probe and drogue were also removed without any problems.

The probe head and the upper damping arm structures were quite warm to the touch when removed (estimated to be 110° to 120° F, 43° to 49° C).The docking probe will be transferred to LM prior to jettison.
Once the probe is out, Stafford opens the LM overhead hatch and removes the docking drogue.
Meanwhile, Young powers down the CSM rendezvous radar transponder, its work now completed.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Ours is the bag that I brought my helmet over in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
The computer is yours when you want it.
Now we've got the load in.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
Roger.
Thank you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And are you fellows in the transfer mode right now?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Just about.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
I've got some PADs for you [garble]...
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: ...have not opened the hatch yet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
I've got some PADs for you, Gene, whenever you're ready to copy and give me a call when you're ready.
Okay?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Joe.
I'll call you when I'm ready.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger that.
And you're aware of where your tool kit is.
Is that affirmative?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Yes.
Affirmative.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
Do you have a LM Delta-P there?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Okay, Joe.
We're going to open the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Roger, Tom.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Comm break.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
We have three additional items that we want brought back.
We want both the cameras brought back and would you believe, we also want the primary canister brought back, the lithium hydroxide canister brought back from the LM.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Where do you plan for us to stow that, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Roger.
We kind of anticipated that, and we're thinking about that right now.
Make that the last item you transfer back, Tom.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: All right.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Comm break.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
We'd like to go to a forward Omni on the High Gain Antenna.
We're just about against the stop now.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Roger.
We are forward Omni.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
Thank you a lot.
Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hello, Houston.
Go ahead and give me the update, would you please?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger that, Gene-o.
Coming to you with APS depletion burn.
Okay for Noun 33: 108:50:31.00; plus 45576, plus five balls, minus 06231; 46000, 4:07; three balls, 251.
Plus 45981, minus five balls, plus 01339.
And all else is NA.
I'm standing by for your readback.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Joe.
APS burn depletion is 108:50:31.00, plus 45576, plus all balls, minus 06231 46000 407.
By the way, who's going to be in here to watch the burn time?
Roll is all zero.
Pitch is 251 986.
Who is going load?
Okay.
Noun 86, plus 45981, and minus all balls, and plus 01339.
And the COAS are NA.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The details of this PAD are:Purpose:(Unmanned) APS burn to depletion.Time of ignition, TIG(Noun 33):108:50:31.00Change in velocity (Noun 81), fps (m/s):X, +4,557.6 (+1,389.2); Y, +000.0 (0.0); Z, -623.1 (-189.9).

The change in velocity is resolved into three components expressed relative to the Local Vertical frame of reference.Total required change in velocity, Delta-VR, fps (m/s):4,600.0 (1,402.0)Burn duration, BT (min:sec):4:07Attitude angles that should be displayed on the FDAI eight ball in the Inertial mode:Roll, 000°; Pitch, 251°.Change in velocity values used to target the AGS (Noun 86), fps (m/s):X + 4598.1 (+ 1401.5), Y -000.0 (0.0), Z + 133.9 (+ 40.8).
These velocities are expressed relative to the Local Vertical frame of reference.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Roger that.
I've got one more change for you, Gene, in your checklist under activation 69, just prior to step 5, we want you to load in 404 plus all zeros.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: I will do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Charlie, does the DAP and everything look good to you right now?
I won't go through that again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
Were you calling?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Yes.
Does the DAP look good to you right now?
I won't go through that again right at the moment.
I'm starting over and running through this real quick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger.
That's good.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And Snoopy, this is Houston.
On the LiOH canister, looks like under the left-hand couch in the sleeping bag is going to be the best place to stow that.
And we really would like to have it come back.
We got a rise in CO2in the LM, and we'd like to take a look at that can.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
I'll try and get it out.
You don't want a new one in do you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Negative that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: This is Apollo Control.
We've completed our shift change in Mission Control.
Flight Director Milton Windler has relieved Flight Director Glynn Lunney, We anticipate the change of shift briefing will begin in about 15 minutes at about 11 pm Central Daylight Time.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Comm break.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Snoopy, this is Charlie Brown.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What do you want, John.
I'm up to my earballs, here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Gene-o, are you on the horn?
Gene-o?
transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: CapCom Engle is instructing Young to use the DAP as specified in the Flight Plan, i.e.
register 1, 61112; register 2, 11001.

tokens: Register 2 code breaks down as shown in the table below.

tokens: The Flight Plan code breaks down to:
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Register 1-CSM & LM (ascent stage only), use quads A&C for X translation, use quads B&D for X translation, ±5.0° deadband, ±0.5° KALCMANU rate.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Register 2-Use quads A&C for roll control, quad A operational, quad B as failed, quad C as failed, quad D operational.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: At107:02:26MCC-H confirmed Young's enquiry about whether the CSM should be in a tight ±0.5° deadband.
This is contrary to the DAP settings detailed above.

tokens: Roger.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay.
We'll monitor.
Thank you.

tokens: You mean the checklist in the Flight Plan there, Joe?

tokens: That's affirmative.

tokens: Man, with those numbers in there, I'm not so - I'm glad I'm getting out.

tokens: Cernan, understandably, is not keen to stay in the LMSnoopywhen it performs the APS burn to depletion manoeuvre, which will place it into solar orbit.

tokens: (Laughter) Roger.

tokens: And just for the record, Joe, you're a fink.

tokens: Copy, fink.

Okay.

tokens: During the undocking prior to solo operations, Young was advised not to use AC roll.
This was due to the problem with the apparent slippage in CM roll (LM Yaw) about the docking interface.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This thing calls me to set your timer, John, but we're well over an hour away, so forget about it.

tokens: Okay.

tokens: Okay, Charlie Brown.

tokens: Charlie Brown, this is Houston.
For your LM weight we want to set in 07544; and, John, we're satisfied with your 61112 setup.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
LM will stay the same because we're going to get rid of that thing,
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's affirmative.

tokens: The AGS DEDA readout 267 displays the magnitude of the LM velocity to be gained in fps in the upcoming APS burn to depletion manoeuvre.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Roger.
Copy 4600.
That looks good, and Snoopy, it looks like you're going to have to slew from Max signal again, and we'll need those knobs - control knobs settings after you do it.

tokens: All right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, this is Charlie Brown.

Houston.

I just wanted to say I sure do thank everybody that worked on that probe and made it work the way it did.

We copy that, John.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Sure is a - sure worked good.

transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Can't tell you how pleased I am.
transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Of the probe, yes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No - you're a fink, I told you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The fink sure is sending a man out to do a boy's job, though.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hey, Joe.
There's slew for a Max signal.
I brought it up a little bit but all I did was move the numbers and they're reading the same thing: 182 and minus 5.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
We copy, Gene-o.
182 and minus 5.
And, as you're coming through the tunnel you might take a look at the docking angle there and see how close it is.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Make that 182 and minus 10.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger.
182 and minus 10.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Charlie Brown, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Go ahead.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger, John.
We got our tail between our legs, here.
We'll need for you to load in your DAP 61102, and you get an "atta boy" for that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: MCC-H eventually agree with the error in the DAP deadband setting, published in the Flight Plan, and confirm to Young that he should use 61102 to set the deadband at 0.5°.

tokens: Alrighty.

tokens: Go ahead.
Over.

tokens: Roger, Snoopy.
When we were...

tokens: Monitoring your activation 69, the last two steps.
We'd like for you to verify that you've put 616 to zeros and 411 to plus 1.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Snoopy, this is Houston.
What we copied down here on the last step was 611 instead of 411.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Yes.
I put 411, I'll check it for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The AGS DEDA address 616 defines whether the LM RCS ullage counter limit is set or not.
The required value for the APS burn to depletion manoeuvre is 0 to prevent any ullage.
AGS DEDA address 411 defines which LM engine will perform the upcoming manoeuvre.
In this case 411 is set to +10000 signifying it is an APS manoeuvre.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Thank you Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: There you go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Snoopy.
We got it.
Thank you very much, and we'll need no ascent feed on this, Snoop.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: MCC-H advise that there will be no need to use the LM APS propellant interconnect to the RCS systems.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okey doke.
I only forgot the PCM switch High one time when I went to update, how's that?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Cernan is referring to the Telemetry PCM switch on panel 12, which at lunar distance has to be set to the Hi position whenever an update is being uplinked to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: That's better than the average bear.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Snoopy, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Go ahead, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gene-o.
Referring back to activation 64 on step 2, we'd like pressure Regs A and B to Egress, please.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Thank you sir.
That one happened in a big fast rush.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You bet you.
Copy that.
Thank you very much Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Engle is checking each step Cernan is carrying out in his preparations for the separation of the LMSnoopy, to ensure nothing gets missed.
He has noticed that the step to place the Pressure Regulator valves A & B in the Egress position has been missed.
This is the usual position during egress or when the cabin is unpressurized.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And Charlie Brown, this is Houston.
I've got a maneuver PAD for you, John, when youre ready to copy.

tokens: Say again, please.

transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
I copy, 182 and minus 5 and our LOS time is 107:31.
We've got about 13 minutes, yet.

transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Go to it.

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger that.
SPS, G&N coming up.
Okay you're Noun 47: 37100, minus 060, plus 079, 119;41;28.85; plus 29472, plus 00558, minus 00165; NA; 071; all else is NA.
And stand by for the readback, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
What is the 071, the pitch angle?
Right?
transcript.speaker: Young

Those are the last two steps.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Say again?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Snoopy.
This is Houston.
Do you copy?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Yes.
I copy, but I didn't hear what you said.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
We need for you to go 616 to all zeros.
This is still activation 69.
This is the last 2 steps: 616 to all zeros and 411 to plus 1.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Babe, I just read it out and it is all zeros.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
Thank you much.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: I'll do it again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
We'll appreciate it if you'll give it one more try.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: That's what I'm doing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The AGS DEDA address 500, displays the velocity to be gained in the X-body axis, in fps.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Those sound good Snoopy.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Roger.
SPS, G&N; 37100; minus 060, plus 079; 119:41;28.85; plus 29472, plus two balls 558, two balls 165; pitch 071.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The PAD is interpreted as follows:Purpose:The PAD is a revised version for an emergency burn to return to Earth at the end of Rev 22.Systems:The burn would be made using the SPS engine, under the control of the Guidance and Navigation system.Pitch and yaw trim (Noun 48):-0.60° and +0.79°.Time of ignition (Noun 33) (hr:min:sec):119:41:28.85.Change in velocity (Noun 81), fps (m/s):X, +2,947.2 (+898.3); Y, +55.8 (+17.0); Z, -16.5 (-5.0).

The large positive number in the X direction implies a large prograde component, essentially adding to their orbital velocity, exactly what would be expected from an escape manoeuvre.Spacecraft attitude:Roll, NA; Pitch, 71°.All the subsequent items on the form are not applicable to this manoeuvre.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Readback is correct, John.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hello, Snoopy.
This is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hello, Snoopy, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger, Snoopy.
We keep losing signal on this High Gain Antenna.
What I'd like for you to do, Gene-o, just before you leave the LM, I'd like for you to slew a Max signal again and give us those settings one more time.
We keep dropping a signal out and rather than going back and trying to slew it, just before you get ready to leave, slew it up again for Max signal, if you would.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: When are you going to have the debriefing in this one, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Glynn [Lunney] said in a couple of days.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: How soon can you shower and be ready?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: That's the first nominal run - That's the first nominal run we ever had.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: You should see those Meds they have up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Houston.
Have you been watching this package A temperature?
We never have got it to go down much all day.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll be with you on that.
Just a minute there, Charlie Brown.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
I guess we've gone this long.
We can go another week or so.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snoopy wants to know if he should go into P42.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Say again Charlie Brown.
I missed part of that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Snoopy would like to know if he should go into P42.
Over.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: P42 is the APS thrusting program.
MCC-H will take remote control of the LGC and command P42 when it is required.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay.
Stand by.
I'll find out.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Charlie Brown, this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Negative, Snoopy.
Leave it in P00.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, just let me verify my guidance control switches for you.
Okay?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger.
Go.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
Guidance control is PNGS.
AGS S-band is Max.
Attitude control is Mode Control on roll, pitch, and yaw and mode control switches both PNGS and AGS are in Auto.
Is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Following on from the issues with the switch settings for the LM staging, which led to the rapid unplanned attitude excursions, Cernan is getting an independent check that he has made the correct settings for the upcoming unmanned APS burn to depletion.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: During the post mission crew debriefing, Cernan mentioned the activities immediately following docking and the preparation for the crew leaving the LM.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"LM stowage for jettison went as planned, with the addition that we loaded some very lightweight but large-volume waste gear from the Command Module on the empty LM pilot's side of the cockpit.
This caused no problems at all.
We transferred the film and the cameras and what have you back to the Command Module and went through the LM closeout procedures as prescribed.
We verified, just before going back, with MSFN that all switches were in order."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: That's correct, Snoopy.
You got them all right.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And you wanted the DSKY left in P00.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's affirmative.
Leave her in P00.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And the updata link switch is On.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
We copy.
And did you copy?
We want you to slew that S-band for Max signal one more time and give us a reading just before you leave.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
I've got one more thing to stow here and then I'll do it.
How much time have I got here yet?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, We've got about 2 minutes and 30 seconds until LOS.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Let me do it now, and I'll take the canister on my way out.
Let me slew this thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That sounds great, Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Hey, Joe, would you believe I've got all the circuit breakers pulled?
And I can't tell, I don't know where MAX signal strength is anymore.
Let me see.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay.
That's good right there, Gene-O, the way you've got it.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Snoopy, Houston TELCOMM says you got her right there.
That's a good shot in the blind.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay.
Same numbers I gave you before.
I'm going off the air.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Roger that.
See you back in Charlie Brown.
Good show, Gene-o.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And we've had Loss Of Signal.

We'll reacquire Apollo 10 in about 46 minutes as the spacecraft comes back around the - onto the front side of the Moon, on its 17th revolution, and during that revolution we'll have the APS burn to depletion and we also have television scheduled, which we hope will give us good coverage of that LM ascent stage maneuver.
The change of shift briefing, we now estimate will be in about 10 minutes.
The participants will be leaving shortly.
At 107 hours, 31 minutes; this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Following LOS, firstly Stafford, then Cernan transferred back to the CM.

They transferred with them the following items for return to Earth:Radiation survey meter16-mm film (6 mags 1 bag)70-mm film (3 mags 1 bag)MonocularFlight data file itemsPersonal preference kits (3)DSEA recorderIn addition.
MCC-H requested the crew bring back the Hasselblad and Maurer cameras, plus the primary LiOH canister.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The crew described the last activities in the LM and the closeout of the docking tunnel, during the post mission crew debriefing.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Cernan, from the1969 Technical debrief:"Then we came back, closed out the LM, and left the tunnel vent valve because we went back to check it in Auto.
So that was it."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Young, from the1969 Technical debrief:"We closed the tunnel, wiped down the seal of the tunnel, put all our gear in the Command Module hatch bag, and shoved it in there.
It took away about 7 or 8 pounds, I guess.
We wiped down both the seal on the spacecraft and the seal around the hatch, and they were both pretty clean.
I expected to get some stuff in there, but we didn't have any way to verify that we had a good pressurization seal.
So we used a Repress system and then we brought the cabin back up to about 5.7 (psia).
We measured the LM Delta-P there for a while.
The CSM/LM Delta-P looked pretty darn good."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The probe and drogue were easily stowed with restraining cables in the left-hand side of the crew station.

The debris, such as used food containers and other disposable items, that had collected in the Command Module over the previous 4 days, was stowed in the hatch stowage bag and secured in the Lunar Module at the right-hand crew station.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The CSM COAS is stowed and the CSM docking target is taken down and transferred to the LM.
A routine fuel cell O2purge is carried out and the CSM VHF is powered down.
Finally, once the CSM forward hatch is reinstalled by the CDR and LMP, the CSM cabin pressure integrity checks are completed.
transcript.speaker: Commentary
